channel.title: Firman Q. S.
channel.description: Recent content on Firman Q. S.

item.description:
• My lonely google scholar

2026

  • Differential evolution algorithm for solving time domain electromagnetics (TDEM) optimization problem, written in Rust

2025

  • Elm, The electricity and magnetism virtual laboratory

item.title: Learning the Subject or Learning the Exam?
item.description:
As a student, I have often wondered whether getting good in education necessarily means learning. The question arose not from reading educational research, but from observing of my friends–asking me on how to prepare for examinations?.

TLDR: A realization that has stayed with me for years began with something surprisingly ordinary: studying with friends before an exam. I used to feel * whenever a friend asked me to study together. I would happily agree, thinking we would spend time understanding the subject from the ground up. When discussing mathematics or physics, I preferred to start from the fundamental concepts and gradually build toward the formulas and procedures. Yet the conversation would often go differently. After a few minutes, they would interrupt and say something along the lines of, “Can we skip this part? Which section is likely to appear on the exam?” or “Just show me the steps needed to solve the question.” I found this attitude disappointing. The goal is clear: maximize marks while minimizing unnecessary effort. From a strategic perspective, this behavior, i can understand it. They may have limited time (or, limited inlaziness), multiple responsibilities, and competitive environments.

item.title: Hacking on XMonad
item.description: Haskell, i think, is the most artistic programming language among other i’ve learned. And, as window manager hoper, there is one written in Haskell, the name already show the signature, “Xmonad”. Originally, this was my old archlinux DTOS configuration, but now i am on fedora (update: i am back home). I have tested on my fedora and it works fine. For other distributions it should be the same, just complete the necessary packages. I’ll try to give a little guidance.

item.title: Euler-Lagrange Equation
item.description:
Kita akan memulai dengan $S$ sebagai integral dari fungsional $f[...]$

$$ S = \int_{x_1}^{x_2} \mathrm{d}x~f[y(x), y'(x), x] \tag{1} $$

di mana $y(x)$ adalah kurva yang belum diketahui, yang menghubungkan ($x_1, y_1$) dan ($x_2, y_2$), sehingga

$$ y(x_1) = y_1 ~~~\text{and}~~~ y(x_2) = y_2\tag{2} $$

Di antara semua kemungkinan kurva yang memenuhi Persamaan (2), kita harus mencari satu kurva yang meminimumkan (atau memaksimumkan, atau setidaknya stasionar) integral $S$. Perhatikan bahwa fungsional $f[...]$ pada Persamaan (1) adalah fungsi dari tiga variabel (variabel dalam konteks fungsional bisa berupa fungsi). Namun, karena integral mengikuti lintasan $y=y(x)$, integrand $f[y(x),y'(x), x]$ adalah fungsi dari satu variabel $x$.

item.title: Plotting Fresnel Equation
item.description:
Introduction: Simple Equation Derivation

We start with Fresnel Equations

$$ \tilde{E}_{0_R} = \left(\frac{\alpha-\beta}{\alpha+\beta}\right)\tilde{E}_{0_I}, ~~~~~~ \tilde{E}_{0_T} = \left(\frac{2}{\alpha + \beta}\right) \tilde{E}_{0_I}. \tag{1} $$

where

$$ \alpha\equiv \frac{\cos\theta_T}{\cos\theta_I}, \tag{2} $$

and

$$ \beta \equiv \frac{\mu_1 v_1}{\mu_2 v_2} = \frac{\mu_1 n_2}{\mu_2 n_1}. \tag{3} $$

If we assume the special case where $\mu_1 \cong \mu_2 \cong \mu_0$ then

$$ \beta \cong \frac{n_2}{n_1}. \tag{4} $$

Based on Snell’s law we know that

$$ \sin\theta_T = \frac{n_1}{n_2} \sin\theta_I. \tag{5} $$

Using identity $\cos^2\theta_T+\sin^2\theta_T = 1$ then the previous $ \alpha $ equation (2) becomes
